title: GPUSparse: GPU-Accelerated Learned Sparse Retrieval with Parallel Inverted Indices
abstract: arXiv:2606.26441v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Learned sparse retrieval models such as SPLADE achieve retrieval quality competitive with dense models while preserving the interpretability and exact-match advantages of sparse representations. However, inference-time scoring still relies on CPU-bound inverted index traversal algorithms (WAND, Block-Max WAND), creating a fundamental bottleneck for real-time serving at scale. We present GPUSparse, a system for GPU-accelerated exact learned sparse retrieval that introduces: (1) a GPU-parallel inverted index with block-aligned, warp-coalesced posting lists; (2) a batched scatter-add scoring algorithm that processes hundreds of queries simultaneously; and (3) fused Triton kernels with an analysis of the tradeoff between work-efficiency and hardware utilization. On MS MARCO passage ranking (8.8M passages) with real SPLADE embeddings, GPUSparse matches CPU exact scoring to three decimals (MRR@10=0.383, equal to Pyserini SPLADE at this precisi

Title: GPUSparse: GPU-Accelerated Learned Sparse Retrieval with Parallel Inverted Indices
Abstract: arXiv:2606.26441v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Learned sparse retrieval models such as SPLADE achieve retrieval quality competitive with dense models while preserving the interpretability and exact-match advantages of sparse representations. However, inference-time scoring still relies on CPU-bound inverted index traversal algorithms (WAND, Block-Max WAND), creating a fundamental bottleneck for real-time serving at scale. We present GPUSparse, a system for GPU-accelerated exact learned sparse retrieval that introduces: (1) a GPU-parallel inverted index with block-aligned, warp-coalesced posting lists; (2) a batched scatter-add scoring algorithm that processes hundreds of queries simultaneously; and (3) fused Triton kernels with an analysis of the tradeoff between work-efficiency and hardware utilization. On MS MARCO passage ranking (8.8M passages) with real SPLADE embeddings, GPUSparse matches CPU exact scoring to three decimals (MRR@10=0.383, equal to Pyserini SPLADE at this precisi
translated_title: GPUSparse：基于GPU加速的并行倒排索引学习型稀疏检索系统
translated_abstract: arXiv:2606.26441v1 公告类型：新论文 摘要：诸如SPLADE之类的学习型稀疏检索模型在保持稀疏表示的可解释性和精确匹配优势的同时，达到了与稠密模型相媲美的检索质量。然而，推理时的评分仍然依赖于CPU上的倒排索引遍历算法（WAND、Block-Max WAND），这成为了大规模实时服务的一个根本性瓶颈。我们提出了GPUSparse，一个用于GPU加速的精确学习型稀疏检索系统，它引入了：（1）一个具有块对齐、线程束合并的帖子列表的GPU并行倒排索引；（2）一个批量的分散-累加评分算法，可同时处理数百个查询；（3）融合的Triton内核，并分析了工作效率与硬件利用率之间的权衡。在MS MARCO段落排序（880万段落）上，使用真实的SPLADE嵌入，GPUSparse在三位小数精度内与CPU精确评分相匹配（MRR@10=0.383，与此精度下的Pyserini SPLADE持平）。
tldr: 本论文提出了GPUSparse，通过GPU并行倒排索引和批处理评分算法，首次实现了与CPU精度一致但速度更快的学习型稀疏检索加速，解决了实时服务中的性能瓶颈。
en_tdlr: This paper presents GPUSparse, which achieves GPU-accelerated learned sparse retrieval with accuracy matching CPU exact scoring by introducing a parallel inverted index and batched scatter-add scoring algorithm, addressing the performance bottleneck in real-time serving.
